profile.personality: curious but reserved; thinks before replying; warms up after a few turns

transcript.content: Hi Priyansh, I'm Miss Maya. Honestly, the first time I heard the song from Sui Dhaaga, I hummed it for days — it felt so warm and honest. How was your movie night with your sister?
We watched Sui Dhaaga too! The music is really catchy. My sister loves Bollywood, so she knows all the songs by heart. By the way, what movie do you like the most?
"Great to hear you liked Sui Dhaaga — I get that, the music adds so much emotion. My sister also hums songs non-stop after a movie! What’s your favourite Bollywood film?"
It's hard to choose just one, but maybe "Dilwale Dulhania Le Jayenge" — it's a classic! My parents watched it again last Diwali, and even I ended up humming some tunes. Have you seen it?
That’s a wonderful choice — I get why it’s a classic, so many people still love it today. The songs are unforgettable. Did you watch it with your sister or alone last time?
We watched it together last Diwali. It's always fun to see the old movies with some new perspective. Oh, I also like watching cricket matches with friends — do you follow sports?
"Diwali with old movies and new joy — I get that, it feels special. Cricket with friends sounds lively, I follow it sometimes. What’s your favourite team?"

I support Mumbai Indians — they’ve had some great moments! Though honestly, any game with my friends feels like a win. We often get too competitive, though, and end up laughing more than cheering. How about you, do you follow any sports?
Mumbai Indians is a strong team — I get the excitement of a good game with friends, it’s all about the fun. Cricket is my go-to sport. What’s your favourite player?
Virat Kohli, for sure. He's a hard worker and a great leader. Sometimes, I watch his interviews — he speaks so clearly. Do you have a favorite player?
Virat Kohli is a fantastic choice — I agree, he plays with heart and speaks with clarity too. I enjoy watching MS Dhoni’s calm style. What’s your take on his leadership?
Dhoni is a legend, no doubt. I like how he stays so calm, even under pressure. It's inspiring. But I think I'll stick with Kohli for now — maybe I'm biased. Cricket is a great way to relax, isn't it?
Dhoni’s calm is a big part of his magic — I get the respect, he’s a true legend. Kohli has his own fire, and that’s inspiring too. Cricket is a great escape — what else do you do to relax?

I like to play video games sometimes, especially when I want to unwind after work. It’s not the same as cricket, but it helps clear my mind. Oh, and I also enjoy listening to Hindi music while cooking—messy but fun. What about you?
Video games and Hindi music while cooking — that sounds like a good mix of fun and calm! I enjoy simple things too. What kind of games do you like?

I mostly play strategy or sports games — FIFA is a favorite, obviously! It’s not the same as the real thing, but it’s close enough. Sometimes I play with friends online, which makes it more exciting. Do you play any games?
"FIFA is a great choice — playing with friends makes it even better, I get that. I don’t play often, but I enjoy watching the excitement. What team do you play for in the game?"
I usually play for Manchester United, just for the fun of it. Even though I'm a Mumbai Indians fan in real life! It's funny how games let you support anyone. Do you have a favorite team in FIFA?
Manchester United in FIFA — that’s a fun pick, it shows you enjoy the game! I don’t play, but I get the appeal. What’s your favorite part of playing FIFA?

The thrill of scoring a last-minute goal is unbeatable! It feels like you're part of the action, even if it's just a game. Sometimes I get so into it, I forget to eat! Do you play games like that too, or are you more into something else?

That thrill of a last-minute goal sounds addictive — I get it, it’s the same with movies that keep you hooked! I don’t play games much, but I enjoy simple things like reading. What’s your favorite book or movie to relax with?

Honestly, I don't read much, but I like watching comedies now and then. Something light, like the movie "Barfi!" — it's funny and has good music. Or sometimes I just stream old Bollywood hits. It's easy to relax that way. Thanks for the chat, Maya — I should get back to work now! Talk to you soon.
"Comedies and old Bollywood hits are perfect for a light unwind — I get that, Barfi! is a great pick. Enjoy your work, and take your time — chat anytime!"

facts.current_learning_focus: AI tools and NLP (chatbots, text analysis)
movie_preferences: Bollywood (especially Zoya Akhtar films and classics like Dilwale Dulhania Le Jayenge), comedies, romantic films; prefers laid-back content on weekends
facts.family: has a sister; watches movies together and collaboratively selects what to watch; sings Bollywood songs with mother

events.what: watched Jugjugg Jeeyo (Bollywood movie); found songs catchy, hummed them all night

moments.text: I enjoy watching movies alone because it gives me freedom to pause or rewind. But I also like laughing with my sister. It's about having that choice.
Old movies feel more real with deeper emotions. Simple, heartfelt stories about ordinary people—like a tailor and his family—feel honest and touching. These stories stay with me.

It felt real and honest. The second one was okay, but I still have a soft corner for the first.")
